title: Sgraffito line-work decorative tile
subtitle: A slip-coated tile with scratched botanical line-work
excerpt: A flat air-dry clay tile coated with coloured slip, then scratched back through the slip layer with a needle tool to reveal botanical line-work. The revealed clay body contrasts with the slip colour. No kiln required.
sourceNotes: Sgraffito technique (scratching through a slip to reveal the clay body) is found across European medieval and Renaissance ceramics. Technique derived from first principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: A surface decoration technique where a top layer (slip, engobe, or paint) is scratched through to reveal the contrasting body or layer beneath.
Clay diluted with water to a brushable, pourable consistency. Used for decorating, joining, and coating.
The drying stage where clay holds its shape but is still damp enough to join, trim, and accept clean sgraffito scratching.

content.text: Sgraffito means scratched in Italian. The technique in this tutorial applies a coloured
slip
over a fresh clay tile, allows it to reach
leather-hard

, and then uses a needle tool to scratch botanical line-work back through the slip to the lighter clay body beneath. The result is a two-tone tile where the scratched lines appear as the natural clay colour against the slip background. No kiln is required; the tile is sealed at the end.

items.name: Black or dark coloured clay slip
items.substitution: Dark acrylic paint diluted 50/50 with water (not as durable but workable)

content.text: Making the tile
Roll air-dry clay to 8 mm thick and cut a square tile 120 mm x 120 mm. Smooth the surface with a damp sponge. Leave the tile on the board.
Applying the slip
Stir the coloured
sgraffito

slip until it is smooth and creamy. It should coat a brush and flow off without dripping. Apply one generous even coat over the top surface of the tile with the mop brush. Work from edge to edge without brushing back over wet slip. Allow the tile to reach leather-hard under a loose tent of plastic: the slip surface should be matte rather than shiny, and the whole tile should feel firm but yielding when lightly touched at the corner. This takes about one to two hours depending on room temperature.
Scratching the design

Sketch a simple botanical design lightly with the needle tool before committing. A single stem with leaves and berries, a small branch with seed heads, or a stylised flower fits within the 120 mm tile. Once satisfied with the sketch, scratch each line confidently with the needle tool, pressing through the slip layer and into the clay by about 1 mm. The scratched line should reveal the lighter clay body as a clean contrast against the dark slip. Work from the centre outward so your hand does not rest on completed lines.
Removing slip crumbs
Once scratching is complete, blow gently across the tile to clear slip crumbs from the scratched channels. Do not rub or wipe; the slip surface is fragile at leather-hard.
Drying and finishing

Allow the tile to dry fully over two days under a loose plastic tent to prevent warping. Once bone-dry, seal the surface with two coats of PVA or acrylic varnish. Attach a self-adhesive tile hanger to the back.
Variations
Use a terracotta-red slip on white clay, or a white slip on red earthenware clay for different contrast combinations.
Try a simple diamond grid instead of plant shapes, for a more geometric look.
Apply two different coloured slips in layers for a three-tone tile. Scratch to different depths to reveal the body colour, first slip, and second slip.

items.symptom: Scratched lines tear and leave ragged edges
items.cause: Slip was too dry when scratching; the slip chipped rather than peeling cleanly
items.fix: Scratch at leather-hard, not bone-dry. If the tile has dried past leather-hard, lightly mist the surface with water and allow it to soften for 10 minutes before scratching

items.symptom: Scratched lines fill back in with slip crumbs
items.cause: Slip crumbs fell back into the channels and were not removed
items.fix: After each line, blow gently across the surface or lift crumbs with the very tip of the needle tool

items.cause: One surface dried faster than the other
items.fix: Dry under a loose plastic tent; place a flat smooth weight on the tile for the last few hours if warping persists
